# Post-Market Debrief — June 17, 2026

Today was a complete washout with zero trades executed, leaving the account flat for the session but down ₹13,918 month-to-date. While a no-trade day isn't inherently bad—sitting out is sometimes the right call—the lack of any opportunities identified or taken suggests the market conditions may have been outside your bot's setup parameters, or signals simply didn't materialize. Without any winning or losing trades to analyze, there's nothing to deconstruct in terms of strategy effectiveness or execution quality. The concerning pattern is the month-to-date deficit, which indicates a deeper issue either with entry logic, position sizing, or risk management that needs to be addressed regardless of today's inactivity. Tomorrow, focus on reviewing whether your bot's filters are too restrictive (eliminating valid setups) or whether market conditions genuinely were unfavorable, then adjust your alert thresholds if you're leaving easy money on the table. If zero-trade days become frequent, consider backtesting your parameters against recent historical data to ensure you're not over-optimizing for a market regime that's already shifted.
